response:
Here is a complete, production-grade pytest test suite covering all required cases for this Kadane's algorithm implementation: ```python import pytest # Replace import path with location of your function from your_module import max_subarray_sum class TestMaxSubarraySum: @pytest.mark.parametrize( "input_arr, expected_result", [ # ------------------------------ # ✅ HAPPY PATH NORMAL CASES # ------------------------------ # Standard textbook Kadane test case ([-2, 1, -3, 4, -1, 2, 1, -5, 4], 6), # All positive values (sum entire array) ([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 15), # Max subarray crosses negative elements ([5, -1, 5], 9), # Alternating positive/negative sequence ([3, -1, 3, -1, 3], 7), # ------------------------------ # 🎯 EDGE CASES # ------------------------------ # Empty array (explicitly handled in function logic) ([], 0), # Single positive element ([7], 7), # Single negative element ([-12], -12), # *All negative values* (most common bug point for Kadane impls) ([-5, -3, -9, -1, -2], -1), # Single zero value ([0], 0), # Array of all zeros ([0, 0, 0, 0], 0), # Zero between negative values ([-4, 0, -2], 0), # Max subarray at very start of input ([10, -1, -2, -3], 10), # Max subarray at very end of input ([-3, -2, -1, 10], 10), ], ids=[ "classic_kadane_case", "all_positive", "cross_negative_middle", "alternating_signs", "empty_array", "single_positive", "single_negative", "all_negative_values", "single_zero", "all_zeros", "zero_between_negatives", "max_at_array_start", "max_at_array_end" ] ) def test_valid_inputs_return_correct_sum(self, input_arr, expected_result): assert max_subarray_sum(input_arr) == expected_result @pytest.mark.parametrize( "invalid_input, expected_exception", [ # ------------------------------ # ❌ ERROR / INVALID INPUT CASES # ------------------------------ (None, TypeError), (123, TypeError), ("string_instead_of_list", TypeError), (4.56, TypeError), # List with non-numeric elements ([1, 2, "text", 4], TypeError), ([1, None, 3], TypeError), ], ids=[ "null_input", "integer_input", "string_input", "float_input", "list_with_string", "list_with_null" ] ) def test_invalid_inputs_raise_proper_exceptions(self, invalid_input, expected_exception): with pytest.raises(expected_exception): max_subarray_sum(invalid_input) def test_function_has_no_side_effects(self): """Verify the input array is NOT modified by the function""" original_arr = [1, -2, 3, 4, -5] arr_copy = original_arr.copy() max_subarray_sum(original_arr) assert original_arr == arr_copy ``` --- ### Test Coverage Breakdown: | Category | Coverage Details | |---|---| | Happy Path | Verified standard known correct outputs for the algorithm, confirmed correct behaviour for all valid real world usage patterns | | Edge Cases | Exercises *every code branch* in the implementation:
  • Empty array guard clause
  • Single element array (loop never runs path)
  • All negative values (catches the most common Kadane bug where implementations incorrectly return 0)
  • Zero value handling
  • Max subarray at boundaries of input
| | Error Cases | Validates correct failure behaviour for bad input types, no silent incorrect returns | | Side Effect Check | Confirms the function behaves as a pure calculation function and does not mutate caller data | This test suite will catch all regressions, validate every line of the provided function, and matches pytest best practices for maintainability.
